After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev 	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/lockmetrics"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:           "work",
		Short:         "Drain the bead execution queue",
		SilenceUsage:  true,
		SilenceErrors: true,
		Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.

ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
bounds.

Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
label; otherwise the label is ignored.

Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
  drained     — no ready beads remain
  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts

work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
`,
		Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
  ddx work

  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  ddx work --once

  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
  ddx work --watch

  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s

  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing

  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: f.runWork,
	}

	// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
	// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
	cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
	cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
	cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
	cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("label-filter", "", "Filter queue selection by label intersection")
	cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
	cmd.Flags().String("attempt-backend", "", "Attempt backend: worktree, local-clone, docker-clone, or in-tree (default: executions.attempt_backend)")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("ignore-cooldown", false, "Ignore retry cooldown across this worker session (requires --reason)")
	cmd.Flags().String("reason", "", "Operator reason required by --ignore-cooldown")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("self-refresh", false, "Force re-exec onto a newer installed ddx binary between iterations (on by default in watch mode; use --no-self-refresh to disable)")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-self-refresh", false, "Disable self-refresh: do NOT re-exec onto a newer installed ddx binary between watch iterations (self-refresh is on by default in watch mode)")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
	cmd.Flags().String("server-managed", "", "Internal: server-managed worker id")
	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkHidden("server-managed")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
	cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
	cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart powerClass)")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("max-recovery-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxRecoveryCostUSD, "Per-bead automated recovery budget in USD for reframe/decompose attempts after repeated ladder exhaustion")
	cmd.Flags().Int("claim-rate-window", agent.DefaultClaimSuccessRateWindow, "Rolling window of recent claim attempts used to compute the claim success rate warning")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("claim-rate-threshold", agent.DefaultClaimSuccessRateThreshold, "Warn when the rolling claim success rate over a full window is at or below this threshold")
	cmd.Flags().Int("preclaim-warn-threshold", agent.DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold, "Consecutive identical pre-claim warn fingerprints across distinct bead IDs required before the loop emits operator attention")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("preclaim-timeout", workguard.DefaultPreClaimTimeout, "Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("route-resolution-timeout", agent.DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout, "Timeout bounding routing preflight and the resolveRoute viability check; on expiry the lease is released and the bead is flagged for operator attention")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
	// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
	cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
		"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
	// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
	// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
	cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
	cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")

	// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
	// "ddx work metrics", "ddx work analyze", and "ddx work status" as
	// subcommands.
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkAnalyzeCommand())
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkStatusCommand())

	return cmd
}

// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
// by this layer.
func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
		return err
	}

	projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
	projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
	lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
	lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
	f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)

	// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
	// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
	// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
	asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
	if !asJSON {
		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
		})
	}

	return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
}

func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
	if asJSON {
		payload := struct {
			ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
			*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
		}{
			ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
			ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
		}
		enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
		return enc.Encode(payload)
	}

	if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
		writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
		writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)

data.text: I’m applying the core implementation now: runtime flag plumbing, the pre-claim fetch/merge step, the post-claim/post-close publish step, and the corresponding warning/operator-attention surfaces. After that I’ll add the focused integration tests against a bare origin.
The code change is in flight. I hit a path typo while patching the loop file, so I’m reapplying against the correct worktree path and then I’ll move straight into the tests.

The implementation is wired. I’m adding tests next: one small CLI flag test for the new enable/disable behavior, then the integration cases that exercise stale origin sync, claim publish visibility, close publish visibility, and the failure-to-operator-attention path.
The new tracker-sync test file needs a cleanup pass: I’m fixing its imports and wiring the log/event sinks correctly before I run the first test slice.
I found the typo: the patch path had a dot instead of the worktree’s dash separator. I’m correcting that and then I’ll run the targeted Go tests to shake out any compile or behavior mismatches.
I’m at verification now. First I’ll run the targeted Go test slice from the bead acceptance, then I’ll fix any compile/test fallout before widening to the pre-commit hook.

data.text: I’ve got the remaining failures narrowed down to claim publication semantics and the failure-path assertion. Next I’m checking the store’s claim behavior and the exact event payloads so I can make the tests line up with the implementation or correct the implementation if it’s actually publishing the wrong state.

The store is the key detail here. I’m reading the claim path now because the test failure suggests the repo’s “claim” contract may already be `open + owner` on disk, while the in-memory API reports `in_progress` only through a different lifecycle transition layer.

data.output:

data.text: I’ve confirmed the store has a real lifecycle-state claim transition. The next read is the exact claim implementation so I can tell whether the test should assert `in_progress` or whether the publish helper is bypassing that state on the on-disk tracker.

I found the mismatch: the loop currently takes the `ClaimWithOptions` path, which only writes the lease sidecar and leaves the tracker row `open`. That explains the `open` vs `in_progress` clone result, so I’m checking whether switching the loop to the row-mutating `Claim` path is safe or whether there’s a narrower way to publish the claimed state.

data.text: I’m at the point of choosing between two fixes: either publish the in-progress status explicitly after a lease-only claim, or switch the loop to the store’s real `Claim` path. I’m checking the test doubles now so I can pick the smaller change that won’t break unrelated loop tests.

I’ve got the fix direction: keep `ClaimWithOptions` so the lease sidecar still carries session/worktree metadata, then explicitly transition the tracked row to `in_progress` when the claim succeeds and tracker sync is enabled. That gives us the published claim state the bead contract asks for without widening the change to every claim path.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green

Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="efa1df917a176163dc85974bf6877a793b610ac5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T023458-5d0e68f2"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="efa1df917a176163dc85974bf6877a793b610ac5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T023458-5d0e68f2"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. - Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="efa1df917a176163dc85974bf6877a793b610ac5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T023458-5d0e68f2"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="efa1df917a176163dc85974bf6877a793b610ac5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T023458-5d0e68f2"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="efa1df917a176163dc85974bf6877a793b610ac5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T023458-5d0e68f2"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, area:federation, kind:feature, kind:reliability, phase:build, spec:FEAT-026</labels>     <metadata base-rev="efa1df917a176163dc85974bf6877a793b610ac5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T023458-5d0e68f2"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. data.text: The package test process is gone, but the hook wrapper shell is still sitting there. I’m reading the hook script now so I can tell whether it’s doing a second hidden step or whether it’s stuck waiting on something the test already spawned.

data.output:
#!/bin/sh
set -u

if [ "$#" -eq 0 ]; then
  exit 0
fi

packages=$(
  printf '%s\n' "$@" |
    xargs -n1 dirname |
    sort -u |
    sed 's|^\./||' |
    sed 's|^cli/||'
)

status=0
for pkg in $packages; do
  [ -d "$pkg" ] || continue
  if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"; then
      status=1
    fi
  fi
done

exit "$status"
